description: Expected parse results for plain text format test fixtures. Plain parser always succeeds - it treats every line as a message.

01_structured.log.note: Plain parser should extract timestamp+level from lines matching its pattern

entries_with_structure.message: Failed to bind to port 443: permission denied
Unable to start server - exiting with code 1

01_structured.log.note_slash_format: Lines with yyyy/MM/dd format may or may not match depending on regex support for slash separator

02_unstructured.log.note: All lines are treated as messages. No structured fields extracted. Parser always succeeds as fallback.

03_nginx.log.note: Nginx access logs. No level keywords, no scanner-parseable timestamps (month-name format). Scanner extracts misleading thread from [timestamp] brackets and spurious logger from HTTP methods. All unstructured from a meaningful extraction standpoint.

04_apache.log.note: Apache HTTPD combined access log + error log. Access log lines have no extractable levels. Error log lines have lowercase level keywords (notice, error, warn) which the scanner's case-sensitive regex does not match. Scanner extracts thread from [Mon ...] brackets and spurious logger from module names before separator.

05_syslog.log.note: Syslog RFC 3164 and RFC 5424 formats. No extractable level keywords (syslog uses facility/severity codes, not text levels). RFC 3164 lines have no scanner-parseable timestamps. RFC 5424 lines have extractable ISO8601 timestamps. Scanner extracts PID brackets as thread for RFC 3164 and structured-data brackets for RFC 5424. Logger extraction picks up spurious words from bracket content area.

06_docker.log.note: Docker/container logs. Docker JSON wrapper lines have extractable levels (INFO/WARN/ERROR) from log content and timestamps from JSON 'time' field. containerd lines with explicit level prefixes (INFO:/ERROR:/WARN:) have extractable levels. Plain container log lines have extractable levels and timestamps. containerd lines without level prefixes (e.g. 'Starting application v1.2.3') have timestamp but no level.

entries.note: Docker JSON wrapper: level from 'log' field content, timestamp from JSON 'time' field
Docker JSON wrapper: WARN from log content, timestamp from JSON 'time' field

entries.message: {"log":"2024-01-15T10:00:02.000Z ERROR connect() failed (111: Connection refused) while connecting to upstream\n","stream":"stderr","time":"2024-01-15T10:00:02.000000003Z"}
entries.note: Docker JSON wrapper: ERROR from log content, timestamp from JSON 'time' field
containerd line without level prefix: has timestamp but no level ('F' is not a level keyword)

entries.message: 2024-01-15T10:00:01.000000000Z stdout F INFO: Connected to database at db-service:5432
2024-01-15T10:00:02.000000000Z stderr F ERROR: Health check failed: /healthz returned 503
2024-01-15T10:00:03.000000000Z stdout F WARN: Memory usage at 85%, considering scale-up

entries.note: Plain container log with timestamp + level
Plain container log with DEBUG level

entries.message: 2024-01-15 10:00:02 INFO  my-app/1.0.0 Connected to PostgreSQL at 10.0.0.5:5432
entries.note: Plain container log with INFO level

entries.message: 2024-01-15 10:00:03 INFO  my-app/1.0.0 Connected to Redis at 10.0.0.6:6379
entries.note: Plain container log with INFO level
Plain container log: scanner finds ' - ' separator in message body, extracting partial message. Level INFO from content.

07_custom_app.log.note: Custom application log formats. Python logging lines have extractable levels (INFO/DEBUG/WARNING->WARN/ERROR) and timestamps. Dotted logger names (urllib3.connectionpool, myapp.module, myapp.auth) are extracted as loggers. Go standard library lines have timestamps but no level keywords. Node.js lines have timestamps but lowercase bracket levels ([info]/[debug]/[warn]/[error]) that the scanner's case-sensitive regex does not match.

entries.note: Python logging: scanner finds ' - ' separator at '00,000 - root', extracting partial message. Level INFO.
Python logging: dotted logger name extracted. Message includes logger+level prefix.

entries.message: myapp.module - WARNING - Deprecated config key found: 'old_timeout', use 'request_timeout'
entries.note: Python logging: WARNING normalized to WARN by scanner.

entries.message: myapp.module - ERROR - Division by zero in calculation: total / count (count=0)
entries.note: Python logging: ERROR level, dotted logger.

entries.message: urllib3.connectionpool - DEBUG - Starting new HTTPS connection (1): api.example.com:443
entries.note: Python logging: urllib3 dotted logger name extracted.

entries.message: myapp.auth - INFO - User 'jdoe' authenticated with role 'admin'
entries.note: Python logging: myapp.auth dotted logger name extracted.
